consecutive-newlines:
indented: "a\n  \nb"
line_tail: "a  \nb"
three: 'a



  b'
two: 'a


  b'
empty-and-space:
empty: ''
leading: ' leading'
multiple: 'tail   '
single: ' '
trailing: 'trailing '
empty-json-schema-property-name:
response_format:
  json_schema:
    name: answer
    schema:
      properties:
        ? ''
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: json_schema
empty-tool-parameter-name:
tools:
- function:
    name: lookup
    parameters:
      properties:
        ? ''
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: function
hash-comment:
messages:
- content: 'hash # comment'
  role: user
response_format: null
json-schema-explicit-long-property-with-:
response_format:
  json_schema:
    name: answer
    schema:
      properties:
        ? aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
          bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: json_schema
long-clean-multiline-ascii:
value: 'This first clean ASCII source line is deliberately longer than eighty columns
  so PyYAML must wrap it automatically without double quotes.

  This second clean ASCII source line is deliberately longer than eighty columns and
  must also wrap automatically without special characters.'
long-clean-multiline-in-array:
items:
- 'This first clean ASCII array line is deliberately longer than eighty columns so
  PyYAML wraps it inside a sequence.

  This second clean ASCII array line is deliberately longer than eighty columns and
  validates item continuation indentation.'
long-clean-multiline-in-message:
messages:
- content: 'This first clean ASCII message line is deliberately longer than eighty
    columns so PyYAML wraps it inside a sequence mapping.

    This second clean ASCII message line is deliberately longer than eighty columns
    and validates continuation indentation.'
  role: user
long-clean-multiline-in-nested-object:
outer:
  inner:
    value: 'This first clean ASCII nested line is deliberately longer than eighty
      columns so PyYAML wraps at the deep mapping level.

      This second clean ASCII nested line is deliberately longer than eighty columns
      and validates deep continuation indentation.'
long-clean-multiline-with-blank-lines:
value: 'This first clean ASCII paragraph is deliberately longer than eighty columns
  so PyYAML must wrap it automatically before the blank paragraph.


  This second clean ASCII paragraph is deliberately longer than eighty columns and
  must wrap automatically after the blank paragraph.'
long-clean-multiline-with-single-quote:
value: 'This clean ASCII line contains don''t and is deliberately longer than eighty
  columns so PyYAML wraps it while escaping the quote.

  This second clean ASCII line contains entity''s description and is deliberately
  longer than eighty columns for another wrap.'
long-json-schema-property-name:
response_format:
  json_schema:
    name: answer
    schema:
      properties:
        ? 
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: json_schema
long-multiline-ascii:
value: "This first source line is deliberately longer than eighty columns so automatic\
  \ wrapping combines with an original newline in PyYAML output.\nThis second source\
  \ line is also deliberately longer than eighty columns and preserves paragraph behavior.\n\
  \n Leading source indentation must remain visible on this line.\nThis source line\
  \ ends with spaces.  "
long-multiline-trailing-newline:
value: 'This clean ASCII source line is deliberately longer than eighty columns so
  PyYAML must wrap it before preserving the trailing newline.

  '
long-multiline-trailing-two-newlines:
value: 'This clean ASCII source line is deliberately longer than eighty columns so
  PyYAML must wrap it before preserving two trailing newlines.


  '
long-nested-string-with-double-spaces:
outer:
  inner:
    value: This long nested scalar reaches a wrapping boundary with two  spaces that
      must survive after PyYAML applies the deeper continuation indentation.
long-plain-ascii:
value: This is a long GraphRAG extraction prompt used to verify PyYAML width wrapping
  behavior across multiple ordinary ASCII words without special scalar indicators
  or punctuation.
long-plain-with-double-spaces:
value: This long plain scalar carries ordinary words until it reaches a wrapping boundary
  with two  spaces that must remain semantically identical after PyYAML serialization.
long-plain-with-triple-spaces:
value: This long plain scalar carries ordinary words until it reaches a wrapping boundary
  with three   spaces that must remain semantically identical after PyYAML serialization.
long-sequence-string-with-double-spaces:
items:
- This long sequence scalar reaches a wrapping boundary with two  spaces that must
  be preserved while PyYAML chooses continuation indentation for the item.
long-single-quoted-ascii:
value: 'This long GraphRAG scalar contains a hash # marker and enough ordinary ASCII
  words to verify PyYAML wrapping while the complete scalar remains enclosed in single
  quotes.'
long-single-quoted-with-double-spaces:
value: 'This long scalar has a hash # marker and reaches a wrapping boundary with
  two  spaces whose exact count must survive inside a single-quoted PyYAML scalar.'
long-string-in-array:
items:
- This long sequence item verifies that PyYAML wraps plain ASCII scalar values using
  the continuation indentation selected for an item nested inside a block sequence.
long-string-in-nested-object:
outer:
  inner:
    value: This long nested mapping value verifies width accounting after every indentation
      level and after the complete mapping key prefix has already occupied columns.
long-string-with-alignment-spaces:
layout:
  value: Column Alpha             Column Beta             Column Gamma continues with
    enough ordinary words to cross the PyYAML preferred width boundary.
long-string-with-leading-continuation-sp:
value: This long scalar reaches the preferred wrapping boundary before a deliberately
  aligned segment    that begins after several meaningful spaces.
long-tool-parameter-name:
tools:
- function:
    name: lookup
    parameters:
      properties:
        ? 
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: function

messages:

    '
  role: user

    type: image_url

nested-metadata:
metadata:
  level:
    items:
    - enabled: true
      tags:
      - x
      - y
nested-multiline-trailing-newline:
outer:
  inner:
    value: 'nested content ends here

      '
nested-sequences:
arrays:
- - a
  - b
- []
- - c
objects:
- a: 1
  b: 2
- nested:
    z: []
strings:
- a
- b
real-extract-graph-request:
max_tokens: 4096
messages:
- content: You extract a knowledge graph from supplied text. Preserve entity names,
    relationship direction, delimiters, paragraph structure, and all Unicode content
    exactly as requested.
  role: system
- content: "-Goal-\nGiven a text document and a list of entity types, identify all\
    \ entities of those types and all clearly related entity pairs. For every entity\
    \ return its name, type, and a comprehensive description. For every relationship\
    \ return the source, target, explanation, and numeric strength.\n\n-Output format-\n\
    Format entities as (\"entity\"<|><name><|><type><|><description>) and relationships\
    \ as (\"relationship\"<|><source><|><target><|><description><|><strength>). Separate\
    \ records with ## and finish with <|COMPLETE|>.\n\nEntity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON,GEO,EVENT\n\
    Text:\n\u897F\u95E8\u5E86\u5728\u5468\u56DB\u4E0E Example Research Institute \u7684\
    \ Martin Smith \u4F1A\u9762\u3002The research institute announced a detailed multi-year\
    \ collaboration involving language models, graph extraction, cache compatibility,\
    \ and reproducible evaluation across several international teams."
  role: user
response_format: null
temperature: 0.0
top_p: 1.0
response-format:
response_format:
  json_schema:
    name: answer
    schema:
      properties: {}
      type: object
    strict: true
  type: json_schema
string-scalars:
a: 'true'
b: 'false'
c: 'null'
d: none
e: 'yes'
f: 'no'
g: 'on'
h: 'off'
i: '123'
j: '1.5'
k: 1e10
l: '2026-07-12'
tools:
tools:
- function:
    description: 'find # value'
    name: lookup
    parameters:
      properties:
        query:
          type: string
      required:
      - query
      type: object
  type: function
trailing-newline-json-schema-property-na:
response_format:
  json_schema:
    name: answer
    schema:
      properties:
        ? 'first

          '
        : type: string
      type: object
  type: json_schema
typed-scalars:
bool_false: false
bool_true: true
float: 1.5
integer: 123
'null': null
